# Using the Secure Socket Layer for Network Communication

**Article**

Establish Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) sessions to facilitate secure communication between client and server.

## Overview

The following terms are used in this discussion:



Most applications need only a few of the functions in this API, which are normally called in the following sequence:

- Prepare for a session
- Call [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLCreateContext(_:_:_:)] to create a new SSL session context.
- Write the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLWriteFunc] and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLReadFunc] I/O functions and register them with Secure Transport by calling the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLSetIOFuncs(_:_:_:)] function.
- Establish a connection using [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CFNetwork], BSD Sockets, or Open Transport. Then call [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLSetConnection(_:_:)] to specify the connection to which the SSL session context applies.
- Call [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLSetPeerDomainName(_:_:_:)] to specify the fully-qualified domain name of the peer to which you want to connect (optional but highly recommended).
- Call [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLSetCertificate(_:_:)] to specify the certificate to be used in authentication (required for server side, optional for client).
- Start a session
- Call [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLHandshake(_:)] to perform the SSL handshake and establish a secure session.
- Maintain a session
- To transfer data over the secure session, Secure Transport calls your [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLWrite(_:_:_:_:)] and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLRead(_:_:_:_:)] functions as needed.
- End a session
- Call [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLClose(_:)] to close the secure session.
- Close the connection and dispose of the connection reference.
- Release the SSL session context by calling [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CoreFoundation/CFRelease].
- If you called `SSLGetPeerCertificates` to obtain any certificates, call [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CoreFoundation/CFRelease] to release the certificate reference objects.

In many cases, it is easier to use the CFNetwork API than Secure Transport to implement a simple connection to a secure (HTTPS) URL. See [https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/Networking/Conceptual/CFNetwork/Introduction/Introduction.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/TP30001132] for documentation of the CFNetwork API and the CFNetworkHTTPDownload sample code for an example of code that downloads data from a URL. If you specify an HTTPS URL, this routine automatically uses Secure Transport to encrypt the data stream.

For functions to manage and evaluate certificates, see [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/certificate-key-and-trust-services].
title: Using the Secure Socket Layer for Network Communication
